summary: Added first-class Nix support for building Melee's main.dol and entering a development shell. The PR introduced pinned Nix sources, package derivations for decomp-toolkit, devkitPPC, mwcc compilers, wibo, and the project build itself, plus GitHub Actions coverage that runs nix-build and validates nix-shell. A small build-system change allowed --build-dtk to point directly at an existing dtk binary, which is important for hermetic Nix builds that provide tools from store paths instead of building them from source in-tree.
what_changed: Added a GitHub Actions job named "Nix" that checks out the repo, pulls the original DOL from the existing ghcr.io/doldecomp/build-melee:main container, installs Nix, enables a Nix cache action, runs nix-build, and verifies nix-shell with a simple command.
Added `.nix/default.nix` to import pinned nixpkgs from `.nix/sources.nix` and define overlays for decomp-toolkit, devkitppc, mwcc, and wibo before calling `.nix/melee.nix`.

Added `.nix/melee.nix` as a Nix derivation for `doldecomp-melee`; it cleans the source tree, provides native build inputs, runs configure.py with explicit Nix store paths for wibo, dtk, sjiswrap, and mwcc, enables parallel building, and installs `build/GALE01/main.dol`.
Added `.nix/shell.nix` to derive a development shell from the Melee derivation and append clang tooling, including clang-tools using llvmPackages_15 and `pkgs.clang.cc.python`.
Added Nix package definitions for decomp-toolkit v0.7.3, devkitPPC r43 wrapper binaries, the GC/Wii compiler bundle, and wibo v0.6.12.
Pinned nixpkgs with `.nix/sources.json` and added a generated `.nix/sources.nix` from Niv.
Added root-level `default.nix` and `shell.nix` as one-line shims importing `.nix` and `.nix/shell.nix` respectively.
Updated `configure.py` help text for `--build-dtk` from "path to decomp-toolkit source" to "path to decomp-toolkit source or binary".

Updated `tools/project.py` so `config.build_dtk_path` can be an existing file path; if it is a file, that path is used as the `dtk` tool directly, otherwise the old source-build path behavior remains.
smart_moves: Made the Nix build hermetic enough to pass explicit tool paths to configure.py instead of relying on environment variables; the PR body notes configure.py does not pick up environment variables the same way the Makefile build does.

Kept decomp-specific external tools as separate Nix derivations, making versions and hashes searchable and reproducible: decomp-toolkit v0.7.3, devkitPPC r43, wibo v0.6.12, sjiswrap v1.1.1, and the 20230715 compiler bundle.
Used overlays in `.nix/default.nix` and `.nix/shell.nix` so project-specific tool packages can be referred to consistently by name in the Melee derivation.
Allowed `--build-dtk` to accept a binary path, which avoids forcing Cargo builds inside the project build when Nix already provides `decomp-toolkit}/bin/dtk`.
Patched the MW linker byte in the Nix compiler derivation with a comment explaining it avoids random crashes under wibo.
Used `lib.cleanSourceWith` in `.nix/melee.nix` to exclude build outputs, expected outputs, `.nix` files, dump files, and object files from the derivation source.
Added CI coverage for both `nix-build` and `nix-shell`, reducing the chance that the Nix environment silently rots.
decomp_lessons: Hermetic build environments should pass exact tool paths into configure scripts rather than depending on host environment discovery, especially for decomp projects where compiler, wrapper, and toolkit versions affect matching.
When packaging decomp toolchains, support both source-tree tool builds and prebuilt/provided tool binaries; `tools/project.py` now treats an existing `--build-dtk` file as the dtk executable.
Keep environment-specific tooling in hidden directories when reviewers request it; here review feedback preferred `.nix` over `nix` for environment utilities.

CI for alternative build environments should still source required game inputs in a legally/operationally consistent way; this job obtains `/orig` from the existing build container before running Nix.
Pinning nixpkgs and fixed-output hashes for external decomp tools makes later matching failures easier to rule out as dependency drift.
A dev shell can reuse the main derivation and override attributes for editor/analysis tooling, reducing divergence between build and development environments.
naming_conventions: Nix environment utilities were placed under a hidden `.nix` directory after review feedback requested `.nix` instead of `nix`.
Nix package files use direct tool names: `.nix/decomp-toolkit.nix`, `.nix/devkitppc.nix`, `.nix/mwcc.nix`, `.nix/wibo.nix`, `.nix/melee.nix`.
The root shims use standard Nix filenames `default.nix` and `shell.nix` for convenient `nix-build` and `nix-shell` entrypoints.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct C/assembly matching changes were present.
The PR indirectly supports matching work by making the exact build toolchain reproducible under Nix.

The mwcc derivation includes a binary patch to `GC/1.1/mwldeppc.exe` to avoid reading garbage from stack and to fix random crashes under wibo; this is a toolchain stability tactic, not a source matching tactic.

The Nix build passes `--wrapper ${wibo}/bin/wibo`, `--build-dtk ${decomp-toolkit}/bin/dtk`, `--sjiswrap ${sjiswrap}`, and `--compilers ${mwcc}` to configure.py so the generated Ninja build uses the packaged tools.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Please move everything to `.nix` instead of `nix`; environment-related utilities should be in hidden directories.
review_feedback.outcome: Changed files in the final slice are under `.nix`, indicating the directory naming feedback was applied.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether the root `default.nix` shim needed to exist and suggested possibly removing it and updating local commands instead.
review_feedback.outcome: The final diff still includes root `default.nix` importing `./.nix`; evidence does not show whether further discussion resolved this.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Accepted the basic idea of handling a direct build_dtk path and said they would clean it up a little.
review_feedback.outcome: Final code uses `config.build_dtk_path.is_file()` to use the path directly, otherwise falling back to the old source-build path.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify `.nix/melee.nix` or update pinned Nix tool versions.
Search for later changes to `configure.py --build-dtk` handling or `tools/project.py` dtk path logic.
Check whether root-level `default.nix` and `shell.nix` shims remained accepted convention after PR 1335.
Look for CI failures or fixes involving the Nix GitHub Actions job.
Search for later comments or issues about the mwldeppc.exe wibo crash patch.
